title: Molten Roasted Russian Mixture Powder Bake
description: A rich, ayurvedic tamasic-friendly brunch dish featuring a molten roasted mixture powder and tempered chocolate. Perfect for a weekend morning treat.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C) and grease a 9-inch round baking dish.
Mix sunflower seeds, mixture powder, and salt in a bowl until well combined.
Transfer the mixture to the prepared baking dish and roast for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
In a separate bowl, melt the tempered chocolate chips over a double boiler or in the microwave in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Remove the baking dish from the oven and pour the melted chocolate over the roasted mixture.
Return the baking dish to the oven and bake for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until the chocolate is set and the mixture is fragrant.
Remove the baking dish from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing into wedges and serving.
Serve warm, garnished with fresh herbs or edible flowers if desired.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving to allow the flavors to meld.
When ready to serve, slice into wedges and serve with a dollop of whipped cream or a sprinkle of powdered sugar if desired.
